title: Living in a Shed Because of River Pollution
summary: A couple in Herefordshire, England, has been living in a shed for years because of river pollution. Jane and Tony Coyle wanted to build a new home, but they had to wait for special permission because the river Lugg is polluted. The pollution comes from farms and sewage. The rules to protect the river have delayed their building plans, costing them a lot of money and causing health problems. They are now part of a legal case against the companies that cause the pollution. Many other people are also affected because new homes cannot be built. The couple hopes their story will help clean up the rivers and allow people to build homes again.

keywords.explanation: dirty things that harm the environment, like chemicals or waste
waste water from toilets and sinks

questions.question: Why have Jane and Tony been living in a shed?
options: They are waiting for permission to build a home
questions.correct_answer: They are waiting for permission to build a home

questions.question: What is causing the river pollution?
How long have the Coyle family been waiting?
What are the Coyles doing about the pollution?
Why did the council stop building near the river?
options: To protect the river from pollution
To make people use less water
questions.correct_answer: To protect the river from pollution

questions.question: How many homes have been stopped because of the rules?

background_read: The river Lugg is in Herefordshire, England. It is a protected river, meaning it is important for nature. Pollution can hurt fish and plants. When rivers are polluted, people cannot build new homes nearby. The Coyles are one family affected by these rules.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jane and Tony Coyle, a couple living in Herefordshire
WHAT: They are living in a shed because they cannot build their home due to river pollution rules
WHERE: In Herefordshire, near the river Lugg
WHY: Pollution from farms and sewage has led to restrictions on building

perspectives.description: They are frustrated and stressed because the delay has cost them money and health.
They stopped building to protect the river from more pollution.
